Metadata.CollectionName: Cast Iron and Brown Rice - Family Recipe Archive
Metadata.Notes: These are the real recipes. Grandma Mabel taught most of these before Hassan left for Cedarbrook. Trying to keep them consistent. Some amounts are estimated because she never measured anything the same way twice.

Recipe.Name: Grandma Mabel's Oxtail Stew

Ingredient.Notes: Ask butcher to cut if not pre-cut. Trim excess fat but leave some for flavor.
Dice rough - it cooks down all the way
Minced or pressed. Mabel used more but this is the measured version.
Optional. Whole in the pot, do not break it open unless you want full heat.
Balances the acid. Mabel added more but keeping it lighter at 1 tbsp when I cook for family.
For searing. Substitute avocado oil if vegetable not available.

Instructions.Step: Season oxtails generously with all-purpose seasoning and black pepper. Let sit 20 minutes if time allows.
Heat oil in cast iron dutch oven or heavy pot over high heat. Sear oxtails in batches, 3-4 minutes per side. Do not crowd - you want brown, not gray. Set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Saute onion in the same pot until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 more minute.
Add tomato paste. Stir and cook 2 minutes until it darkens slightly.
Return oxtails to pot. Add broth, Worcestershire, brown sugar, thyme, carrots, celery, and scotch bonnet if using.
Bring to boil, then reduce to low simmer. Cover and cook 3 to 3.5 hours, turning oxtails halfway through. Meat should fall from bone.
Taste and adjust salt before serving. Remove scotch bonnet whole before serving.
Serve over white rice or with bread. Mabel always served with cornbread.

Recipe.Notes: Tastes better the next day. Freeze leftovers in portions - this is meal prep gold for $25 total. Best protein per dollar ratio in the collection.

Recipe.Name: Collard Greens

Ingredient.Notes: Strip leaves from stem by folding and pulling. Stems go in compost or trash - not the pot.

Ingredient.Item: Smoked turkey leg or smoked turkey neck
Ingredient.Notes: Mabel switched from pork neckbones to smoked turkey years back for a lighter pot. Same result, less saturated fat.
Add at the end. Brightens everything up.

Instructions.Step: Wash collards thoroughly. Strip leaves and cut or tear into rough pieces.
In a large pot, saute onion until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add turkey leg and broth. Bring to boil.
Add collards in batches - they will wilt down significantly. Pack them in.
Season with salt, pepper, red pepper if using. Reduce heat to low simmer.
Cover and cook 1 to 1.5 hours until greens are very tender and pot likker has developed.
Add apple cider vinegar in the last 5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Remove turkey leg, shred meat, return to pot or serve alongside.

Recipe.Notes: The pot likker (liquid in the pot) is nutritious - do not drain. Serve it with the greens. Goes with everything.

Recipe.Name: Skillet Cornbread

Ingredient.Notes: Mabel said no sugar. I add 1 tablespoon. She was not wrong but this is how I make it now.
Substitute: 1.25 cups regular milk plus 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, let sit 5 minutes

Instructions.Step: Preheat oven to 425F. Put a 10-inch cast iron skillet in the oven while it heats.
Mix all dry ingredients in a bowl. Make a well in the center.
Whisk eggs, buttermilk, and oil together in a separate bowl.
Pour wet into dry. Mix only until combined - lumps are okay. Do not overmix.
Carefully remove skillet from oven. Add a small amount of oil or butter and swirl to coat.
Pour batter into the hot skillet. It should sizzle.
Bake 20-25 minutes until top is golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Let cool 5 minutes before cutting. Serve directly from the skillet.

Recipe.Notes: The hot skillet is the whole secret. That is what gives it the crust. Mabel made this almost every Sunday. The cast iron was hers - I do not have it at school but a regular oven-safe pan works okay.

Ingredient.Notes: About $1.50/lb at Aldi, sometimes less. Do not use boneless - they dry out.
Regular paprika works but smoked gives better color and flavor

Instructions.Step: Preheat oven to 425F. Line a sheet pan with foil (easier cleanup).
Pat chicken dry with paper towels. This step matters - moisture is the enemy of crispy skin.
Mix all spices and oil in a small bowl to form a paste.
Rub paste all over chicken including under the skin where possible.
Place skin-side up on sheet pan. No need to flip.
Bake 40-45 minutes until skin is golden brown and internal temp is 165F.
Rest 5 minutes before serving. The skin should be crispy.

MealPrepNotes.Note: Cook 8 thighs at once for the week. Reheat in oven at 375F for 10 minutes - not microwave, the skin gets soft.
Total cost for 8 thighs: approximately $6-7 at Aldi. That is 4 meals for about $1.65 per serving.
Pairs with brown rice and collards or roasted vegetables from the same meal prep batch.
